apps.name: ollama
apps.description: Get up and running with large language models.
apps.fullDescription:
## IMPORTANT NOTE ##
This is a shared app. Once installed by the Olares Admin, all users in the cluster can use it through reference app.

## OVERVIEW ##
Ollama is a user-friendly interface for running large language models (LLMs) locally. It is a valuable tool for researchers, developers, and anyone who wants to experiment with language models. With Ollama, you can easily download and run LLMs, customize and create your own models, and chat with your LLMs using files on your device.

Ollama supports a wide range of models, including:
- Llama 3 8B
- Llama 3 70B
- Phi 3 Mini 3.8B
- Phi 3 Medium 14B
- Gemma 2 9B
- Gemma 2 27B
- Mistral 7B
- Moondream 2 1.4B
- Neural Chat 7B
- Starling 7B
- Code Llama 7B
- Llama 2 Uncensored 7B
- LLaVA 7B
- Solar 10.7B
apps.upgradeDescription:
Upgrade Ollama version from v0.17.7 to v0.20.3

For detailed release notes including new models and features in each version, please visit:
https://github.com/ollama/ollama/releases

apps.name: bge-reranker-v2-m3
apps.title: BGE Reranker v2 M3 (Ollama)
apps.description: Lightweight multilingual reranker (1.2 GB) optimized for RAG pipelines with strong Chinese-English performance.
apps.fullDescription:
## IMPORTANT NOTE ##
This model runs on CPU only. If you want to run it on GPU, please install the generic version of Ollama program.

This is a shared app. Once installed by the Olares Admin, all users in the cluster can use it through reference app.

## MODEL OVERVIEW ##
BGE-Reranker-v2-M3 is a lightweight reranking model developed by BAAI (Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence), optimized based on BGE-M3-0.5B architecture. It is designed for multilingual retrieval tasks and provides efficient context reranking capability for RAG workflows.

The model filters irrelevant content and prioritizes relevant passages to improve accuracy and response speed in generative AI applications. It is especially strengthened for mixed Chinese-English scenarios.

**Note:** As of June 2025, Ragflow 0.19 and Dify don't support Ollama reranking models. Alternatives include FastGPT or vLLM deployment.

## Model Details
- Model Name: BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3
- Base: BGE-M3-0.5B
- Size: 1.2GB
- Context Length: 8192 tokens
- Task: Text Reranking
apps.modelReadme:
BGE-Reranker-v2-M3 is a lightweight reranking model from Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence (BAAI), optimized based on BGE-M3-0.5B architecture, designed for multilingual retrieval tasks with enhanced performance in mixed Chinese-English scenarios. It filters irrelevant content and prioritizes relevant passages to improve accuracy and response speed for generative AI.

Note: As of June 2025, Ragflow 0.19 and Dify don't support Ollama reranking models. Alternatives include FastGPT or vLLM deployment.

apps.name: qwen3-reranker-8b
apps.title: Qwen3 Reranker 8B (Ollama)
apps.description: 8B reranker with Q4_K_M quantization (5.0 GB) — top accuracy for multilingual RAG reranking across 100+ languages.
apps.fullDescription:
## IMPORTANT NOTE ##
This model runs on CPU only. If you want to run it on GPU, please install the generic version of Ollama program.

This is a shared app. Once installed by the Olares Admin, all users in the cluster can use it through reference app.

## MODEL OVERVIEW ##
Qwen3-Reranker-8B is a text reranking model from Alibaba's Qwen team, built on the Qwen3-8B-Base architecture. It is designed for ranking and reordering search results, evaluating document relevance to queries across diverse applications.

The model supports 100+ languages with instruction-aware capabilities for task customization. It ranks among top performers in multilingual retrieval benchmarks (MTEB, CMTEB, MMTEB).

**Note:** As of June 2025, Ollama doesn't fully support reranking models in Ragflow/Dify. Alternatives include FastGPT or vLLM deployment.

## QUANTIZATION ##
This model uses **Q4_K_M** quantization (5.0 GB). Quantization compresses model weights from 16-bit to 4-bit precision, reducing memory usage ~4x with minimal quality loss. The K-quant method keeps critical layers at higher precision.

## Model Details
- Model Name: Qwen/Qwen3-Reranker-8B
- Parameters: 8B
- Quantization: Q4_K_M (5.0GB)
- Context Length: 32768 tokens
- Task: Text Reranking
- Languages: 100+
apps.modelReadme:
Qwen3-Reranker-8B is a text reranking model from Alibaba's Qwen team, built on the Qwen3-8B-Base architecture. It supports 100+ languages with instruction-aware capabilities for task customization. It ranks among top performers in multilingual retrieval benchmarks (MTEB, CMTEB, MMTEB).

Five quantized versions available: Q3_K_M (4.1GB), Q4_K_M (5.0GB), Q5_K_M (5.8GB), Q8_0 (8.7GB), F16 (16GB). Q5_K_M or Q4_K_M recommended for most users.

Note: As of June 2025, Ollama doesn't fully support reranking models in RAGflow, Dify, and FastGPT. Alternatives include API-based or vLLM deployment.

apps.name: ollamabgem3567mv2
apps.title: BGE-M3 (Ollama)
apps.description: Multi-functional, multi-lingual, multi-granularity embedding model for search and RAG.
apps.fullDescription:
## IMPORTANT NOTE ##
This model runs on CPU only. If you want to run it on GPU, please install the generic version of Ollama program.

This is a shared app. Once installed by the Olares Admin, all users in the cluster can use it through reference app.

## MODEL OVERVIEW ##
BGE-M3 is a new model from BAAI distinguished for its versatility in Multi-Functionality, Multi-Linguality, and Multi-Granularity. Based on the XLM-RoBERTa architecture:

- **Multi-Functionality**: Simultaneously performs dense retrieval, multi-vector retrieval, and sparse retrieval
- **Multi-Linguality**: Supports more than 100 working languages
- **Multi-Granularity**: Processes inputs from short sentences to long documents of up to 8192 tokens

## Model Details
- Model Name: BAAI/bge-m3
- Parameters: 567M
- Dimension: 1024
- Sequence Length: 8192
- Reference: Chen et al., "BGE M3-Embedding: Multi-Lingual, Multi-Functionality, Multi-Granularity Text Embeddings Through Self-Knowledge Distillation" (arXiv:2402.03216, 2024)
apps.modelReadme:
BGE-M3 is a new model from BAAI distinguished for its versatility in Multi-Functionality, Multi-Linguality, and Multi-Granularity.

- Multi-Functionality: It can simultaneously perform the three common retrieval functionalities of embedding model: dense retrieval, multi-vector retrieval, and sparse retrieval.
- Multi-Linguality: It can support more than 100 working languages.
- Multi-Granularity: It is able to process inputs of different granularities, spanning from short sentences to long documents of up to 8192 tokens.

Reference: Chen et al., "BGE M3-Embedding: Multi-Lingual, Multi-Functionality, Multi-Granularity Text Embeddings Through Self-Knowledge Distillation" (arXiv:2402.03216, 2024)

apps.name: gemma4-31b
apps.title: Gemma 4 31B
apps.description: Google's dense 31B multimodal model with 256K context, vision, tool use, and frontier-level reasoning.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW ##
Gemma 4 is a family of multimodal models by Google DeepMind designed to deliver frontier-level performance. The 31B dense variant offers Google's strongest open-weight reasoning with a massive 256K token context window.

## Key Features
- **256K Context Window**: Process extremely long documents, codebases, and conversations
- **Dense Architecture**: All 30.7B parameters active for maximum quality per token
- **Multimodal**: Accepts text and image inputs
- **Tool/Function Calling**: Native support for autonomous agents
- **Thinking Mode**: Configurable chain-of-thought reasoning with control tokens
- **140+ Languages**: Broad multilingual support
- **Strong Reasoning**: Top-tier performance on math, logic, and coding benchmarks (MMLU Pro: 85.2%, AIME 2026: 89.2%)

## Specifications
- Parameters: 30.7B (dense)
- Context Length: 256K tokens
- Architecture: Dense transformer
- Quantization: Q4_K_M (default)
- Disk Size: ~20GB

## Use Cases
- Complex reasoning and analysis
- Long document processing and summarization
- Multilingual chat and translation
- Code generation and review
- Agentic workflows with tool calling
- Image understanding
apps.modelReadme:
Gemma 4 is a family of multimodal models by Google DeepMind designed to deliver frontier-level performance. They excel at reasoning, agentic workflows, coding, and multimodal understanding with text and image processing capabilities.

The 31B dense model features configurable thinking modes with control tokens, native function-calling support for autonomous agents, and a 256K token context window. It supports 140+ languages and offers top-tier performance on reasoning benchmarks.

apps.name: qwen35-27b
apps.title: Qwen 3.5 27B
apps.description: Alibaba's flagship 27B multimodal model with vision, thinking, tools, and 256K context across 201 languages.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW ##
Qwen 3.5 27B is a flagship model from Alibaba's latest Qwen generation, integrating breakthroughs in multimodal learning, architectural efficiency, and reinforcement learning.

## Key Features
- **Unified Vision-Language Foundation**: Early fusion training on multimodal tokens
- **Efficient Hybrid Architecture**: Gated Delta Networks combined with sparse MoE
- **Scalable RL Generalization**: Robust real-world adaptability via million-agent environments
- **201 Languages**: Expanded global linguistic coverage
- **256K Context**: Native 262K tokens, extensible to 1M+
- **Vision**: Multimodal vision-language understanding
- **Tool Calling**: Agent capabilities with function calling
- **Thinking Mode**: Chain-of-thought reasoning

## Specifications
- Parameters: 27B
- Context Length: 256K tokens (native), extensible to 1M+
- Architecture: Hybrid transformer with Gated Delta Networks + sparse MoE
- Disk Size: ~17GB (Q4_K_M)

## Use Cases
- Advanced chat and conversation
- Complex reasoning and analysis
- Multilingual communication (201 languages)
- Code generation and debugging
- Document analysis and summarization
- Agentic workflows with tool calling
apps.modelReadme:
Qwen3.5 is a family of open-source multimodal models delivering exceptional utility and performance across text and vision tasks with support for 201 languages and dialects.

Key breakthroughs include unified vision-language foundation via early fusion training, efficient hybrid architecture using Gated Delta Networks with sparse Mixture-of-Experts, scalable RL across million-agent environments, and near-100% multimodal training efficiency.

The 27B variant supports native 262K token context (extensible to 1M+), vision understanding, tool calling, and thinking mode for chain-of-thought reasoning.

apps.modelReadme:
Qwen3 is the latest generation of large language models in Qwen series, offering a comprehensive suite of dense and mixture-of-experts (MoE) models. Built upon extensive training, Qwen3 delivers groundbreaking advancements in reasoning, instruction-following, agent capabilities, and multilingual support.

The Qwen3-30B-A3B is a Mixture-of-Experts model with 30B total parameters and ~3B active parameters per forward pass, providing an excellent balance of capability and efficiency. It supports a 256K context window and over 100 languages.

Key features:
- Hybrid thinking modes: toggle between fast responses and deep chain-of-thought reasoning
- Native tool/function calling for agent workflows
- MoE architecture for efficient inference

Welcome to the gpt-oss series, OpenAI's open-weight models designed for powerful reasoning, agentic tasks, and versatile developer use cases.

apps.name: deepseek-r1-32b
apps.title: DeepSeek R1 32B
apps.description: Best open reasoning model. Excels at math, logic, and complex problem solving.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW ##
DeepSeek-R1 is a state-of-the-art reasoning model that has demonstrated outstanding performance across mathematics, programming, and general logic benchmarks. The 32B distilled version brings powerful reasoning to a single GPU.

## Key Features
- **Superior Reasoning**: Top-tier performance on math and logic tasks
- **Chain-of-Thought**: Transparent step-by-step reasoning process
- **128K Context**: Large context window for complex problems
- **Distilled**: Knowledge distilled from the full 671B model
- **Competitive**: Performance approaching O3 and Gemini 2.5 Pro

## Specifications
- Parameters: 32B (distilled from 671B)
- Context Length: 128K tokens
- Disk Size: ~20GB
- Quantization: Q4_K_M

## Use Cases
- Mathematical reasoning and proofs
- Complex logic puzzles
- Code generation and debugging
- Scientific analysis
- Multi-step problem solving
apps.modelReadme:
DeepSeek-R1 is a family of open reasoning models with performance approaching that of leading models such as O3 and Gemini 2.5 Pro. The 32B distilled version brings powerful reasoning to a single GPU.

The model received a version upgrade to DeepSeek-R1-0528 with significantly improved reasoning and inference. Performance benchmarks show strong results in mathematics, programming, and general logic.

Distilled from the full 671B model using 800k reasoning samples, demonstrating that larger model reasoning patterns transfer effectively to smaller dense models.

Key features:
- Superior reasoning on math, coding, and logic tasks
- Transparent chain-of-thought reasoning process
- 128K context window
- Tool calling support
- MIT License

apps.modelReadme:
QwQ is the reasoning model of the Qwen series. Compared with conventional instruction-tuned models, QwQ can achieve significantly enhanced performance in downstream tasks, especially hard problems.

QwQ-32B is the medium-sized reasoning model, capable of achieving competitive performance against state-of-the-art reasoning models, e.g., DeepSeek-R1, o1-mini.

The model represents Qwen's initial exploration of scaling Reinforcement Learning to enhance reasoning, with future work focused on combining stronger foundation models with RL and exploring agent integration for long-horizon reasoning.

Key features:
- Deep reasoning with extended thinking chains
- Tool calling support
- 40K context window
- Competitive with DeepSeek-R1 and o1-mini
- 32B dense parameters

apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW ##
Phi-4 Reasoning is Microsoft's reasoning-enhanced version of Phi-4, specifically tuned for complex reasoning tasks. Built upon Phi-4's strong foundation, it adds dedicated reasoning capabilities while remaining compact at 14B parameters.

## Key Features
- **Reasoning-Tuned**: Specifically optimized for chain-of-thought reasoning
- **Compact Size**: 14B parameters fits easily in GPU memory
- **Strong Math**: Excellent performance on mathematical reasoning
- **Fast Inference**: Quick responses for interactive use
- **Efficient**: Great performance-per-parameter ratio

## Specifications
- Parameters: 14B
- Context Length: 16K tokens
- Disk Size: ~11GB
- Architecture: Dense transformer

## Use Cases
- Mathematical reasoning and problem solving
- Logic puzzles and analysis
- Code reasoning and debugging
- Resource-efficient reasoning workloads
- Educational applications
apps.modelReadme:
Phi-4 Reasoning is a 14-billion parameter open-weight reasoning model that rivals much larger models on complex reasoning tasks.

Phi-4 reasoning was created through supervised fine-tuning using OpenAI's o3-mini demonstrations. It consistently outperforms the base Phi-4 model and exceeds DeepSeek-R1 Distill Llama 70B (5x larger), showing competitive performance against significantly larger models such as DeepSeek-R1.

Benchmarks cover mathematical reasoning, scientific reasoning, long-context QA, instruction following, coding, knowledge understanding, safety detection, and general capabilities.

A reasoning-plus variant (phi4-reasoning:plus) is also available, adding reinforcement learning training for further improvements.

Key features:
- Reasoning-tuned via o3-mini demonstrations
- 32K context window
- Outperforms models 5x its size on reasoning tasks
- Available in standard and plus (RL-enhanced) variants

- **Breakthrough Efficiency**: Complete model family trained for <$3.5M total cost
- **Toggle Thinking**: Switch reasoning on/off based on task complexity

## Specifications
- Parameters: 32B
- Context Length: 128K tokens
- Disk Size: ~20GB
- Architecture: Dense transformer with hybrid reasoning

## Use Cases
- Complex reasoning with efficiency
- General-purpose AI assistant
- Coding and analysis
- Tasks requiring adaptive thinking depth
- Production reasoning workloads
apps.modelReadme:
Cogito v1 Preview is a family of hybrid reasoning models by Deep Cogito that outperform the best available open models of the same size across most standard benchmarks.

Built on Iterated Distillation and Amplification (IDA), Cogito models don't just search longer -- they develop better intuition through self-improvement, paving the way to superintelligence.

The models feature hybrid reasoning: toggle between direct fast answering and deep thinking subroutine via system prompt. They produce 60% shorter reasoning chains than DeepSeek R1 with superior performance.

Key features:

- Tool calling support

apps.name: nemotron3-nano-4b
apps.title: Nemotron-3 Nano 4B
apps.description: NVIDIA's efficient 4B hybrid MoE model with reasoning and tool-use capabilities.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW
Nemotron 3 Nano is NVIDIA's compact language model designed for both reasoning and non-reasoning tasks. The 4B variant features a hybrid Mixture-of-Experts architecture with 23 Mamba-2/MoE layers plus 6 Attention layers, achieving strong performance in a small footprint.

## Key Features
- **Thinking Mode**: Generates reasoning traces before final responses
- **Tool/Function Calling**: Native support for structured tool interactions
- **Hybrid MoE Architecture**: 128 experts + 1 shared expert per MoE layer, 6 experts activated per token
- **256K Context Window**: Extended context for complex tasks
- **Multilingual**: English, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese

## Specifications
- Parameters: 4B (active: 3.5B)
- Context Length: 256K tokens
- Architecture: Hybrid Mamba-2/MoE + Attention
- Quantization: Q4_K_M (default, 2.8GB)
apps.modelReadme: Nemotron 3 Nano is an LLM trained by NVIDIA designed for both reasoning and non-reasoning tasks. It generates reasoning traces before providing final responses, with configurable reasoning output. The architecture employs a hybrid Mixture-of-Experts design with 23 Mamba-2/MoE layers plus 6 Attention layers. Each MoE layer contains 128 experts plus 1 shared expert, with 6 experts activated per token. Supported languages: English, German, Spanish, French, Italian, and Japanese.

apps.name: all-minilm
apps.title: All-MiniLM
apps.description: Ultra-tiny embedding model. Only 46MB, perfect for lightweight RAG on any hardware.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW
All-MiniLM is a tiny, ultra-fast embedding model based on the Sentence Transformers framework. At only 46MB, it can run on virtually any hardware while providing useful semantic embeddings for sentence-level datasets using self-supervised contrastive learning.

## Key Features
- **Ultra-Tiny**: Only 46MB, smallest practical embedding model
- **Blazing Fast**: Near-instant embedding generation
- **Low Resources**: Runs on any CPU, even edge devices
- **Good Quality**: Reasonable embedding quality for its size
- **Battle-Tested**: One of the most widely used embedding models (2.9M+ pulls)

## Specifications
- Parameters: ~22M (L6-v2) / ~33M (L12-v2)
- Max Sequence Length: 512 tokens
- Embedding Dimension: 384
- Disk Size: 46MB (22m) / 67MB (33m)

## Use Cases
- Lightweight RAG pipelines
- Edge device deployment
- Rapid prototyping
- High-throughput embedding
- Resource-constrained environments

**Note:** Requires Ollama 0.1.26 or later. This model can only be used to generate embeddings.
apps.modelReadme:
Embedding models on very large sentence level datasets.

The project aims to train sentence embedding models on very large sentence level datasets using a self-supervised contrastive learning objective. This model can only be used to generate embeddings.

**Note:** This model requires Ollama 0.1.26 or later.

## Usage

### REST API
```
curl http://localhost:11434/api/embeddings -d '{
  "model": "all-minilm",
  "prompt": "The sky is blue because of Rayleigh scattering"
}'
```

### Python
```python
ollama.embeddings(model='all-minilm', prompt='The sky is blue because of Rayleigh scattering')
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
ollama.embeddings({ model: 'all-minilm', prompt: 'The sky is blue because of Rayleigh scattering' })
```

## MODEL OVERVIEW
Qwen3-VL 30B is the most powerful vision-language model in the Qwen family to date, with significant improvements in text understanding, visual reasoning, spatial understanding, and video comprehension. This 30B MoE model (3B active parameters) delivers flagship-level multimodal performance.

## Key Features

- **Superior Text-Centric Performance**: Matches Qwen3-235B flagship on text tasks through joint pretraining
- **Visual Coding**: Generate code from images or videos (mockups to HTML/CSS/JS)
- **Spatial Understanding**: 2D and 3D grounding for complex spatial reasoning
- **256K Context**: Natively supports 256K tokens, expandable to 1M tokens
- **Multimodal Reasoning**: Thinking variant optimized for STEM and math
- **OCR in 32 Languages**: Expanded from 10 to 32 supported OCR languages

- Modalities: Text + Images + Video
- Default Size: ~20GB (Q4_K_M)
apps.modelReadme:
Qwen3-VL is the most powerful vision-language model in the Qwen model family to date. In this generation, there are improvements to the model in many areas: its understanding and generating text, perceiving and reasoning about visual content, supporting longer context lengths, understanding spatial relationships and dynamic videos, or interacting with AI agents.

## Key Features
- **Visual Agent Capabilities**: Operate computer/mobile interfaces, recognize GUI elements, and call tools. Achieves top global performance on benchmarks like OS World.
- **Superior Text-Centric Performance**: Joint pretraining of text and visual modalities matches Qwen3-235B-A22B-2507 flagship language model performance.
- **Greatly Improved Visual Coding**: Generate code from images or videos — turning design mockups into Draw.io, HTML, CSS, or JavaScript code.
- **Much Better Spatial Understanding**: 2D grounding with relative coordinates and 3D grounding capabilities for complex spatial reasoning and embodied AI.
- **Long Context & Long Video Understanding**: Natively supports 256K tokens of context, expandable up to 1 million tokens.
- **Stronger Multimodal Reasoning**: Thinking variant specially optimized for STEM and math reasoning.
- **Better OCR Across More Languages**: OCR now supports 32 languages (increased from 10).

## MODEL OVERVIEW
MiniCPM-V 2.6 is the latest and most capable model in the MiniCPM-V series from OpenBMB. Built on SigLip-400M and Qwen2-7B with 8B total parameters, it surpasses GPT-4o mini, GPT-4V, Gemini 1.5 Pro, and Claude 3.5 Sonnet on single image understanding.

## Key Features
- **Leading Performance**: Average score of 65.2 on OpenCompass across 8 benchmarks
- **Multi-Image Understanding**: Conversation and reasoning over multiple images
- **Strong OCR**: Processes images up to 1.8M pixels, surpasses GPT-4o on OCRBench

apps.modelReadme:
MiniCPM-V 2.6 is the latest and most capable model in the MiniCPM-V series. The model is built on SigLip-400M and Qwen2-7B with a total of 8B parameters. It exhibits significant performance improvement over MiniCPM-Llama3-V 2.5, introducing new features for multi-image and video understanding.

## Leading Performance
Achieves an average score of 65.2 on OpenCompass (8 popular benchmarks). With 8B parameters, it surpasses GPT-4o mini, GPT-4V, Gemini 1.5 Pro, and Claude 3.5 Sonnet for single image understanding.

## Multi-Image Understanding and In-context Learning
Performs conversation and reasoning over multiple images. State-of-the-art performance on Mantis-Eval, BLINK, Mathverse mv, and Sciverse mv benchmarks.

## Strong OCR Capability
Processes images with any aspect ratio up to 1.8 million pixels (1344x1344). Surpasses GPT-4o, GPT-4V, and Gemini 1.5 Pro on OCRBench. Supports English, Chinese, German, French, Italian, Korean.

## Superior Efficiency
Produces only 640 tokens processing 1.8M pixel images (75% fewer than most models), improving inference speed, latency, memory, and power consumption.

**Requires Ollama 0.3.10 or later.**

apps.name: glm-ocr
apps.title: GLM OCR
apps.description: Multimodal 0.9B OCR model for complex document understanding — text, table, and figure recognition with 128K context.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW
GLM-OCR is a multimodal optical character recognition model for complex document understanding, built on the GLM-V encoder-decoder architecture. It integrates the CogViT visual encoder pre-trained on large-scale image-text data, a lightweight cross-modal connector with efficient token downsampling, and a GLM-0.5B language decoder.

## Key Features
- **State-of-the-Art Performance**: Achieves 94.62 on OmniDocBench V1.5, ranking #1 overall
- **Real-World Optimized**: Robust on complex tables, code-heavy documents, seals, and unconventional layouts
- **Efficient Inference**: Only 0.9B parameters — supports vLLM, SGLang, and Ollama deployment
- **Easy to Use**: Fully open-sourced with comprehensive SDK and inference toolchain

## Capabilities
- **Text Recognition**: `ollama run glm-ocr "Text Recognition: ./image.png"`
- **Table Recognition**: `ollama run glm-ocr "Table Recognition: ./image.png"`
- **Figure Recognition**: `ollama run glm-ocr "Figure Recognition: ./image.png"`

## Specifications
- Parameters: 0.9B
- Context Length: 128K
- Input: Text + Image
- Default Size: 2.2GB (BF16)
apps.modelReadme:
GLM-OCR is a multimodal optical character recognition model designed for sophisticated document analysis. Built on the GLM-V encoder-decoder framework, it integrates the CogViT visual encoder trained on extensive image-text datasets, a streamlined cross-modal connector featuring efficient token downsampling, and a GLM-0.5B language decoder.

## Key Capabilities
- **Text recognition**: `ollama run glm-ocr "Text Recognition: ./image.png"`
- **Table recognition**: `ollama run glm-ocr "Table Recognition: ./image.png"`
- **Figure recognition**: `ollama run glm-ocr "Figure Recognition: ./image.png"`

## Distinguishing Features

**State-of-the-Art Performance**: Achieves 94.62 score on OmniDocBench V1.5, ranking first overall.

**Real-World Optimization**: Purpose-built for practical enterprise applications, maintaining consistent performance on intricate tables, code-dense documents, seals, and unconventional layouts.

**Computational Efficiency**: Operating with merely 0.9B parameters, supports vLLM, SGLang, and Ollama deployment options.

apps.modelReadme:
Qwen3 is the latest generation of large language models in Qwen series, offering a comprehensive suite of dense and mixture-of-experts (MoE) models. Built upon extensive training, Qwen3 delivers groundbreaking advancements in reasoning, instruction-following, agent capabilities, and multilingual support.

Key features include significantly enhanced reasoning capabilities surpassing previous QwQ and Qwen2.5 instruct models on mathematics, code generation, and commonsense logical reasoning. The model demonstrates superior human preference alignment, excelling in creative writing, role-playing, multi-turn dialogues, and instruction following. It has expertise in agent capabilities, enabling precise integration with external tools in both thinking and unthinking modes, and supports 100+ languages and dialects with strong multilingual instruction following and translation capabilities.

apps.name: mistral-small-24b
apps.title: Mistral Small 24B
apps.description: Mistral's 24B model with native function calling, multilingual support, and agent-centric design.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW
Mistral Small 3 sets a new benchmark in the small LLM category below 70B, featuring 24B parameters with capabilities rivaling much larger models. It is exceptionally knowledge-dense, fitting in a single RTX 4090 or a 32GB RAM MacBook once quantized.

## Key Features
- **Native Tool Use**: Best-in-class agentic capabilities with native function calling and JSON output
- **Multilingual**: Covers dozens of languages including English, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Dutch, and Polish
- **Advanced Reasoning**: State-of-the-art conversational and reasoning capabilities
- **32K Context Window**: Efficient context handling
- **Strong System Prompt Adherence**: Reliable instruction following
- **Tekken Tokenizer**: 131K vocabulary for efficient tokenization

## Specifications
- Parameters: 24B
- Context Length: 32K tokens
- Default Size: 14GB (Q4_K_M)
- Quantization: Q4_K_M

## Use Cases
- Fast response conversational agents
- Low latency function calling
- Subject matter expert fine-tuning
- Local inference for sensitive data
- Fraud detection, customer triaging, on-device control
apps.modelReadme:
Mistral Small 3 sets a new benchmark in the 'small' Large Language Models category below 70B, featuring 24B parameters with state-of-the-art capabilities. The model is exceptionally knowledge-dense, fitting in a single RTX 4090 or a 32GB RAM MacBook once quantized.

Ideal use cases include fast response conversational agents, low latency function calling, subject matter experts via fine-tuning, and local inference for hobbyists and organizations handling sensitive data. The model features multilingual support covering dozens of languages, best-in-class agentic capabilities with native function calling and JSON output, advanced conversational and reasoning capabilities, and strong system prompt adherence. It uses the Tekken tokenizer with 131K vocabulary.

Industry applications span fraud detection in financial services, customer triaging in healthcare, on-device command and control in robotics/automotive/manufacturing, plus virtual customer service and sentiment analysis across sectors.

apps.name: dolphin3-8b
apps.title: Dolphin 3 8B
apps.description: Uncensored 8B model with function calling, coding, and math. No content restrictions.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW
Dolphin 3.0 Llama 3.1 8B is the next generation of the Dolphin series of instruct-tuned models designed to be the ultimate general purpose local model. It supports coding, math, agentic functions, function calling, and general use cases.

## Key Features
- **Uncensored**: No artificial content restrictions or refusals
- **Function Calling**: Native tool use and API interaction
- **128K Context**: Large context window for complex tasks
- **8B Parameters**: Compact and fast
- **Local Control**: Full control over system prompts, model versions, alignment tuning, and data privacy
- **Versatile**: Supports coding, math, agentic functions, and general tasks

## Specifications
- Parameters: 8B
- Context Length: 128K tokens
- Default Size: 4.9GB (Q4_K_M)
- Architecture: Llama 3.1 based

## Use Cases
- Unrestricted AI assistance
- Tool use and function calling
- Code generation and math
- Creative writing without limitations
- Custom AI agent building with full local control
apps.modelReadme:
Dolphin 3.0 Llama 3.1 8B is the next generation of the Dolphin series of instruct-tuned models designed to be the ultimate general purpose local model. It supports coding, math, agentic functions, function calling, and general use cases.

Unlike cloud-based alternatives that maintain control of the system prompt, deprecating and changing things as they wish, Dolphin prioritizes user autonomy. With a local model you maintain control over the system prompt, you decide which version, you can tune the alignment to your own preferences, and your data stays private.

apps.name: medgemma-27b
apps.title: MedGemma 27B
apps.description: Google's 27B medical text reasoning model. Strong on MedQA, MedMCQA, and clinical benchmarks.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW
MedGemma 27B is a text-only medical language model from Google, built on the Gemma 3 architecture. It is specifically trained and fine-tuned for clinical reasoning, medical text understanding, and biomedical question answering.

## Key Features
- **Medical Text Specialization**: Trained on diverse medical and biomedical datasets
- **Strong Benchmarks**: MedQA 89.8%, MedMCQA 74.2%, PubMedQA 76.8%, MMLU Med 87.0%
- **27B Parameters**: Large enough for complex medical analysis
- **128K Context**: Long context window for processing medical documents
- **Instruction-Tuned**: Optimized for medical question answering and reasoning

## Specifications
- Parameters: 27B (text-only)
- Context Length: 128K tokens
- Default Size: 17GB
- Architecture: Gemma 3 based, decoder-only transformer with GQA

## Important Notice
This model is intended for research and educational purposes. The outputs are not intended to directly inform clinical diagnosis, patient management decisions, or treatment recommendations. Always consult qualified healthcare professionals.

## Use Cases
- Medical question answering
- Clinical note summarization
- Biomedical research assistance
- Medical text analysis
- Health information extraction
apps.modelReadme:
MedGemma is a collection of Gemma 3 variants trained for medical text and image comprehension, designed to help developers build healthcare-based AI applications. The 27B variant is text-only and instruction-tuned, focusing on medical text reasoning.

Performance on medical benchmarks: MedQA 89.8% (best-of-5) / 87.7% (0-shot), MedMCQA 74.2%, PubMedQA 76.8%, MMLU Med 87.0%, MedXpertQA 26.7%, AfriMed-QA 84.0%.

The model was trained on diverse public datasets including MIMIC-CXR, Slake-VQA, PAD-UFES-20, SCIN, TCGA, CAMELYON, PMC-OA, and proprietary de-identified datasets covering radiology, ophthalmology, dermatology, and pathology. All datasets were rigorously de-identified to protect patient privacy.

IMPORTANT: The outputs generated by MedGemma are not intended to directly inform clinical diagnosis, patient management decisions, treatment recommendations, or any other direct clinical practice applications.

apps.name: medgemma-4b
apps.title: MedGemma 4B
apps.description: Google's 4B medical multimodal model. Analyze medical images and text with vision support.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW
MedGemma 4B is Google's compact medical multimodal model built on Gemma 3, capable of understanding both medical text and medical images. It incorporates a SigLIP image encoder pre-trained on de-identified medical imagery including chest X-rays, dermatology, ophthalmology, and histopathology images.

## Key Features
- **Medical Multimodal**: Process both medical text and images (chest X-rays, dermatology, pathology, ophthalmology)
- **Compact Size**: Only 4B parameters, 2.5GB on disk
- **Strong Imaging Benchmarks**: MIMIC CXR 88.9 F1, DermMCQA 71.8%, PathMCQA 69.8%
- **128K Context**: Long context window
- **Instruction-Tuned**: Optimized for medical visual question answering

## Specifications
- Parameters: 4B
- Modalities: Text + Medical Images
- Default Size: 2.5GB
- Architecture: Gemma 3 based multimodal with SigLIP encoder
- Images normalized to 896x896 resolution

## Important Notice
This model is intended for research and educational purposes. The outputs are not intended to directly inform clinical diagnosis, patient management decisions, or treatment recommendations.

## Use Cases
- Medical image analysis (X-rays, dermatology, pathology, ophthalmology)
- Clinical text understanding
- Medical visual question answering
- Health research assistance
- Medical education tools
apps.modelReadme:
MedGemma is a collection of Gemma 3 variants trained for medical text and image comprehension, designed to help developers build healthcare-based AI applications. The 4B variant is multimodal, incorporating a SigLIP image encoder pre-trained on de-identified medical imagery.

Imaging benchmarks: MIMIC CXR 88.9 F1 (vs 81.1 baseline), CheXpert CXR 48.1 F1 (vs 31.2), DermMCQA 71.8% (vs 42.6%), SlakeVQA 62.3 F1 (vs 38.6), VQA-Rad 49.9 F1 (vs 38.6), PathMCQA 69.8% (vs 37.1%).

The model was trained on public datasets including MIMIC-CXR, Slake-VQA, PAD-UFES-20, SCIN, TCGA, CAMELYON, PMC-OA, Mendeley Digital Knee X-Ray, and proprietary de-identified datasets covering radiology CT, ophthalmology fundus, dermatology, and pathology histopathology.

IMPORTANT: The outputs generated by MedGemma are not intended to directly inform clinical diagnosis, patient management decisions, treatment recommendations, or any other direct clinical practice applications.

apps.name: translategemma-12b
apps.title: TranslateGemma 12B
apps.description: Google's 12B translation model built on Gemma 3. Supports 55 languages with vision input.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW
TranslateGemma is a new collection of open translation models built on Gemma 3, helping people communicate across 55 languages. The 12B model offers a good balance of translation quality and speed.

## Key Features
- **55 Languages**: Broad language pair support including major world languages and many regional variants
- **Vision Input**: Supports both text and image inputs for translation
- **Translation-Specialized**: Purpose-built for high-quality, natural translation
- **128K Context**: Large context window for document-level translation
- **12B Parameters**: Good balance of quality and speed

## Specifications
- Parameters: 12B
- Supported Languages: 55
- Default Size: 8.1GB (Q4_K_M)
- Architecture: Gemma 3 based
- Context Length: 128K tokens
- Input: Text + Images

## Prompt Format
The model expects a structured prompt identifying source and target languages with language codes, requesting translation without additional commentary. Two blank lines must precede the text to translate.

## Use Cases
- Document translation
- Real-time chat translation
- Website localization
- Multilingual content creation
- Cross-language communication
- Image-based translation
apps.modelReadme:
TranslateGemma is a new collection of open translation models built on Gemma 3, helping people communicate across 55 languages. Available in 4B, 12B, and 27B parameter sizes, the models support both text and image inputs with a 128K context window.

The model expects a structured prompt template identifying source and target languages with language codes. Two blank lines must precede the text to translate.

Supported languages include: English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch, Russian, Polish, Turkish, Arabic, Hebrew, Persian, Hindi, Bengali, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Malayalam, Thai, Vietnamese, Japanese, Korean, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Afrikaans, Amharic, Azerbaijani, Bulgarian, Catalan, Czech, Danish, Estonian, Finnish, Filipino, Georgian, Greek, Gujarati, Hausa, Hungarian, Icelandic, Indonesian, Irish, Kazakh, Latvian, Lithuanian, Macedonian, Malay, Maltese, Nepali, Norwegian, Oriya, Romanian, Serbian, Slovak, Slovenian, Somali, Swahili, Swedish, Ukrainian, Urdu, Uzbek, Welsh, Yoruba, and Zulu.

apps.name: qwen35-9b
apps.title: Qwen 3.5 9B
apps.description: Balanced 9B multimodal model with vision, thinking, tools, and 256K context across 201 languages.
apps.fullDescription:
## MODEL OVERVIEW ##
Qwen3.5 9B is a balanced mid-range model from Alibaba's latest generation, offering a great mix of coding ability, vision understanding, and general-purpose performance with the latest hybrid architecture.

## Key Features
- **256K Context**: Native 256K token window
- **Vision**: Multimodal vision-language understanding
- **Tool Calling**: Agent capabilities with function calling
- **Thinking Mode**: Chain-of-thought reasoning
- **Efficient Hybrid Architecture**: Gated Delta Networks with Gated Attention (32 layers)
- **201 Languages**: Broadest multilingual coverage
- **Mid-Range Size**: Great speed-quality tradeoff

## Specifications
- Parameters: 9B
- Context Length: 256K tokens
- Hidden Dimension: 4096
- Layers: 32 (hybrid with Gated DeltaNet + Gated Attention)
- Disk Size: ~6.6GB (Q4_K_M)

## Use Cases
- General-purpose AI assistant
- Code generation and review
- Image understanding
- Multilingual communication (201 languages)
- Document analysis
- Agentic workflows with tool calling
apps.modelReadme:
Qwen3.5 is a family of open-source multimodal models delivering exceptional utility and performance across text and vision tasks with support for 201 languages and dialects.

The 9B variant features a hybrid architecture with Gated DeltaNet and Gated Attention across 32 layers, 4096 hidden dimension, and native 256K token context. It supports vision understanding, tool calling, and thinking mode for chain-of-thought reasoning.

Key breakthroughs include unified vision-language foundation via early fusion training, efficient hybrid architecture, and scalable RL generalization.

apps.name: openwebui
apps.description: User-friendly WebUI for LLMs
apps.fullDescription:
Open WebUI(Formerly Ollama WebUI) is an extensible, feature-rich, and user-friendly self-hosted WebUI designed to operate entirely offline. It supports various LLM runners, including Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. For more information, be sure to check out our Open WebUI Documentation.

Key Features of Open WebUI
- Ollama/OpenAI API Integration: Effortlessly integrate OpenAI-compatible APIs for versatile conversations alongside Ollama models. Customize the OpenAI API URL to link with LMStudio, GroqCloud, Mistral, OpenRouter, and more.

- Pipelines, Open WebUI Plugin Support: Seamlessly integrate custom logic and Python libraries into Open WebUI using Pipelines Plugin Framework. Launch your Pipelines instance, set the OpenAI URL to the Pipelines URL, and explore endless possibilities. Examples include Function Calling, User Rate Limiting to control access, Usage Monitoring with tools like Langfuse, Live Translation with LibreTranslate for multilingual support, Toxic Message Filtering and much more.

- Responsive Design: Enjoy a seamless experience across Desktop PC, Laptop, and Mobile devices.

- Progressive Web App (PWA) for Mobile: Enjoy a native app-like experience on your mobile device with our PWA, providing offline access on localhost and a seamless user interface.

- Full Markdown and LaTeX Support: Elevate your LLM experience with comprehensive Markdown and LaTeX capabilities for enriched interaction.

- Hands-Free Voice/Video Call: Experience seamless communication with integrated hands-free voice and video call features, allowing for a more dynamic and interactive chat environment.

- Model Builder: Easily create Ollama models via the Web UI. Create and add custom characters/agents, customize chat elements, and import models effortlessly through Open WebUI Community integration.

- Native Python Function Calling Tool: Enhance your LLMs with built-in code editor support in the tools workspace. Bring Your Own Function (BYOF) by simply adding your pure Python functions, enabling seamless integration with LLMs.

- Local RAG Integration: Dive into the future of chat interactions with groundbreaking Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) support. This feature seamlessly integrates document interactions into your chat experience. You can load documents directly into the chat or add files to your document library, effortlessly accessing them using the # command before a query.

- Web Search for RAG: Perform web searches using providers like SearXNG, Google PSE, Brave Search, serpstack, serper, Serply, DuckDuckGo and TavilySearch and inject the results directly into your chat experience.

- Web Browsing Capability: Seamlessly integrate websites into your chat experience using the # command followed by a URL. This feature allows you to incorporate web content directly into your conversations, enhancing the richness and depth of your interactions.

- Image Generation Integration: Seamlessly incorporate image generation capabilities using options such as AUTOMATIC1111 API or ComfyUI (local), and OpenAI's DALL-E (external), enriching your chat experience with dynamic visual content.

- Many Models Conversations: Effortlessly engage with various models simultaneously, harnessing their unique strengths for optimal responses. Enhance your experience by leveraging a diverse set of models in parallel.

- Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): Ensure secure access with restricted permissions; only authorized individuals can access your Ollama, and exclusive model creation/pulling rights are reserved for administrators.

- Multilingual Support: Experience Open WebUI in your preferred language with our internationalization (i18n) support. Join us in expanding our supported languages! We're actively seeking contributors!

- Continuous Updates: We are committed to improving Open WebUI with regular updates, fixes, and new features.
apps.title: Open WebUI

apps.name: codeserver
apps.description: VS Code in the browser
apps.fullDescription:
Run VS Code on any machine anywhere and access it in the browser.

Highlights:
- Code on any device with a consistent development environment
- Use cloud servers to speed up tests, compilations, downloads, and more
- Preserve battery life when you're on the go; all intensive tasks run on your server
- VS Code features are supported but not all extensions

code-server is an open-source project that transforms Visual Studio Code (VS Code) into a server, enabling it to run on any machine. Developers can access VS Code from any device with an internet connection, opening up new possibilities for remote development.

code-server stands out for its compatibility with most VS Code extensions. This feature allows developers to tailor their coding environment to their specific needs, even when working remotely.
apps.title: Code Server

apps.name: context7
apps.description: Up-to-date library documentation for AI coding assistants
apps.fullDescription:
Context7 is an MCP server by Upstash that provides up-to-date, version-specific documentation and code examples directly from source to your AI coding assistant.

Instead of relying on outdated training data, Context7 fetches current library documentation on demand. This resolves common issues where AI models hallucinate APIs, use deprecated patterns, or generate generic code based on stale knowledge.

Features:
* Provides up-to-date documentation for thousands of libraries
* Works with any MCP-compatible AI coding tool (Cursor, Claude Code, VS Code Copilot, etc.)
* Fetches version-specific docs and code examples from source
* Runs as an HTTP MCP server accessible on port 8080
* No database or GPU required — lightweight and simple
apps.title: Context7

apps.name: crawl4ai
apps.description: Open-source LLM Friendly Web Crawler & Scraper
apps.fullDescription:
Crawl4AI turns the web into clean, LLM ready Markdown for RAG, agents, and data pipelines. Fast, controllable, battle tested by a 50k+ star community.

# Features
Markdown Generation
- Clean Markdown: Generates clean, structured Markdown with accurate formatting.
- Fit Markdown: Heuristic-based filtering to remove noise and irrelevant parts for AI-friendly processing.
- Citations and References: Converts page links into a numbered reference list with clean citations.
- BM25 Algorithm: Employs BM25-based filtering for extracting core information and removing irrelevant content.

Structured Data Extraction
- LLM-Driven Extraction: Supports all LLMs (open-source and proprietary) for structured data extraction.
- Chunking Strategies: Implements chunking (topic-based, regex, sentence-level) for targeted content processing.
- CSS-Based Extraction: Fast schema-based data extraction using XPath and CSS selectors.
- Schema Definition: Define custom schemas for extracting structured JSON from repetitive patterns.

Browser Integration
- Managed Browser: Use user-owned browsers with full control, avoiding bot detection.
- Remote Browser Control: Connect to Chrome Developer Tools Protocol for remote, large-scale data extraction.
- Session Management: Preserve browser states and reuse them for multi-step crawling.
- Proxy Support: Seamlessly connect to proxies with authentication for secure access.
- Multi-Browser Support: Compatible with Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit.

Crawling & Scraping
- Dynamic Crawling: Execute JS and wait for async or sync for dynamic content extraction.
- Screenshots: Capture page screenshots during crawling for debugging or analysis.
- Comprehensive Link Extraction: Extracts internal, external links, and embedded iframe content.
- Caching: Cache data for improved speed and to avoid redundant fetches.
- Full-Page Scanning: Simulates scrolling to load and capture all dynamic content.

Deployment
- Dockerized Setup: Optimized Docker image with FastAPI server for easy deployment.
- Secure Authentication: Built-in JWT token authentication for API security.
- Scalable Architecture: Designed for mass-scale production and optimized server performance.
apps.title: Crawl4AI

apps.name: opencode
apps.description: The open source AI coding agent.
apps.fullDescription:
OpenCode is an open-source agent that helps you write code in the terminal, IDE, or desktop.

- Support LSPs to automatically load appropriate LSPs for LLMs
- Multi-session Launch multiple agents in parallel in the same project
- Share Link Share any session link for reference or debugging
- GitHub Copilot Sign in with GitHub to use your Copilot account
- ChatGPT Plus/Pro Login with OpenAI to use your ChatGPT Plus or Pro account

Usage

apps.name: n8n

n8n is an extendable workflow automation tool. With a fair-code distribution model, n8n will always have visible source code, be available to self-host, and allow you to add your own custom functions, logic and apps. n8n's node-based approach makes it highly versatile, enabling you to connect anything to everything.

# Available integrations
n8n has 200+ different nodes to automate workflows. The list can be found on: https://n8n.io/integrations

# Build powerful workflows, really fast
- Handle really complex processes
- Simple debugging
- Use templates to get started fast
- Code when you need it, UI when you don't
- Reuse your work

# Get peace of mind
- Connect to any system
- Customize error handling
- Horizontal scaling
- Host on your own infrastructure

# Features
- Visual workflow editor
- Run JavaScript
- npm package import
- Workflows can wait
- Conditional logic and branching
- Webhook triggers
- Retry failed executions
- Multi-trigger workflows
- Source code available
- User management
- Create custom nodes
- Encrypted credential store
- Copy + paste workflows
- Export workflows to JSON
